choice: Add a Liability entity {type: mortgage|loan|other, current balance, interest rate, optional amortization schedule / monthly payment, optional link to a real-estate Holding}. In NET WORTH it nets against assets as a negative line (assets − liabilities). In the FORECAST it is held-flat or amortized down per its schedule, but its payment is NOT a separate engine cashflow — it's already part of the real spending schedule (fb2dfbc8), so we avoid double-counting. Liabilities are never 'drawn down' or invested. This keeps v1 honest about net worth (a core deliverable) without inventing a debt-service engine.
rationale: The objective makes 'track actual net worth' central, and net worth is meaningless without debts. Real estate is already a non-depleting net-worth line (af999d56) but its mortgage has no home today — a household with a $1M house and an $800k mortgage would show $1M, badly overstating net worth and the bequest distribution (ff44dae1). Modeling the payment inside spending rather than as its own cashflow matches how the spreadsheet (source) treats outflows and avoids a second cashflow path.
